title_translated: MindEdit-Bench：基于真实照片的视觉语言模型对象级反事实空间推理基准测试
summary_translated: 现有视觉语言模型（VLMs）的基准测试大多检验观察性空间推理：模型描述输入中已可见的关系。现有的“如果……会怎样”类任务通常只改变观察者而保持场景不变。VLM能否预测假设性移动或旋转某个物体的后果？我们引入MindEdit-Bench，一个由三张智能手机拍摄的室内场景三联图构建的六个空间推理任务基准，通过自动化的野外3D场景图提取管道生成。四个任务探测对观测结构的感知与视角变换；两个新任务L4（空间编辑）和L5（跨视图可见性编辑）探测对象级反事实推理，正确答案在所有输入图像中均不存在。每个问题提供8-24个结构化选项，支持答案字母级别的空间错误与备用错误诊断。该基准涵盖120个未公开的私有室内场景，不来自公共数据集，降低了公共数据预训练重叠风险。在15个VLM上对1,003个经人工验证的问题进行评估，任务平均准确率仅为8%-31%，而人类多数投票准确率为81%-97%。人类与最佳VLM之间的综合差距达53个百分点，且每项任务至少存在39个百分点的差距。结构化答案空间进一步揭示了非均匀失败模式，包括较弱的相机深度轴推断能力以及在困难可见性编辑案例中的备用行为。

summary: Large Vision-Language Models (LVLMs) rely extensively on Visual Instruction Tuning (VIT) to elicit their multimodal reasoning capabilities. However, we find a discrepancy: VIT often packs multiple language tasks about the same image for conversational, multi-turn training, whereas existing benchmarks evaluate LVLMs in isolated, single-turn scenarios. The models can suffer from visual attention decay and contextual overfitting during multi-turn training, making it hard for them to realize their full potential in the mismatched test phase. To close the gap, we propose learning with Stochastic Turn Depth (StochasT), which stochastically groups language tasks for the same image into clusters of varying sizes (turn depth) while preserving their organic order. Hence, while StochasT draws on Dropout and stochastic depth for ResNets, it does not actually drop anything to maximize the utility of the training data. Furthermore, we introduce a challenging, benchmark-agnostic evaluation mechanism based on the Balanced Latin Square to measure LVLMs' robustness under varying contextual dependencies. Extensive experiments demonstrate that StochasT effectively grants LVLMs strong, harmonized capabilities for both single-turn and multi-turn use cases.
title: StochasT: Learning with Stochastic Turn Depth for Visual Instruction Tuning
comment: Accepted to ECCV 2026. Project page and code: this https URL
title_translated: StochasT：基于随机回合深度的视觉指令微调学习
summary_translated: 大型视觉语言模型（LVLMs）广泛依赖视觉指令微调（VIT）来激发其多模态推理能力。然而我们发现存在一个差异：VIT通常将多个关于同一图像的语言任务打包用于对话式、多回合训练，而现有基准则在孤立的单回合场景下评估LVLM。模型在多回合训练中可能遭受视觉注意力衰减和上下文过拟合，导致在不匹配的测试阶段难以发挥全部潜力。为弥合这一差距，我们提出基于随机回合深度（StochasT）的学习方法，该方法随机将同一图像的语言任务聚类为不同规模（回合深度）的组，同时保留其自然顺序。因此，尽管StochasT借鉴了Dropout和ResNet中的随机深度思想，但并未实际丢弃任何内容，以最大化训练数据的利用率。此外，我们引入了一种基于平衡拉丁方的挑战性、基准无关的评估机制，用于衡量LVLM在不同上下文依赖下的鲁棒性。大量实验表明，StochasT能有效赋予LVLM在单回合和多回合使用场景下强大且协调的能力。

summary: Current molecular generation benchmarks emphasize task complexity, molecule novelty, and property alignment; they largely overlook a critical concern: the potential safety risks of AI-generated molecules. In practice, many generative models may produce molecules with toxic, reactive, or otherwise hazardous characteristics - posing hidden dangers that remain insufficiently addressed. To address this gap, we introduce MolSafeEval, a benchmark dedicated to evaluating and analyzing the safety risks of molecular generation. Unlike prior approaches that rely on narrow toxicity predictors, MolSafeEval integrates heterogeneous safety knowledge - ranging from toxicological databases to hazard rules - into a structured molecular safety knowledge graph. This graph serves as a foundation for large language model-based reasoning, enabling systematic detection and explanation of unsafe features in generated compounds. We further categorize molecular generative models into four representative task types - unconditional generation, property optimization, target protein-based design, and text-based generation - and provide standardized datasets and safety evaluation protocols for each. By systematically revealing the safety vulnerabilities of current generative approaches, MolSafeEval offers a new lens for benchmarking molecular models and provides essential guidance toward safer, more trustworthy molecular design.
title: MolSafeEval: A Benchmark for Uncovering Safety Risks in AI-Generated Molecules
title_translated: MolSafeEval：揭示AI生成分子安全风险的基准测试
summary_translated: 当前分子生成基准测试强调任务复杂性、分子新颖性和属性一致性；却很大程度忽视了一个关键问题：AI生成分子的潜在安全风险。实际上，许多生成模型可能产生具有毒性、反应性或其他危险特性的分子——带来尚未充分解决的隐藏风险。为填补这一空白，我们引入MolSafeEval，一个专门用于评估和分析分子生成安全风险的基准测试。不同于以往依赖狭窄毒性预测器的方法，MolSafeEval将异构安全知识——从毒理学数据库到危害规则——整合进一个结构化的分子安全知识图谱中。该图谱成为基于大语言模型推理的基础，支持对生成化合物中不安全特征的系统性检测与解释。我们进一步将分子生成模型分为四类代表性任务类型——无条件生成、属性优化、基于目标蛋白的设计以及基于文本的生成，并为每类提供标准化数据集和安全评估协议。通过系统揭示当前生成方法的安全漏洞，MolSafeEval为分子模型的基准测试提供了新视角，并为更安全、更可信的分子设计提供了必要指导。

summary: Understanding how complex cognitive functions are organized within artificial systems is central to interpreting large language models (LLMs) and relating them to biological cognition. Yet although LLMs exhibit broad cognitive-like behaviours, it remains unclear whether their internal representations form reproducible functional systems that explain behaviour, failure and links to human cognition. Here we present NeuroCogMap, a cognitive neuroscience-inspired framework that organizes internal features of LLMs into functional parcels and links them to interpretable functions, cognitive capabilities and a cognitive hierarchy. These parcels form a stable and semantically coherent organization that is partly conserved across models and functionally linked to model outputs. Within this organization, major LLM failures, including hallucination, bias, refusal failure and sycophancy, correspond to distinct disruptions in representational and behavioural-control systems, yielding internal signatures for mechanism-guided detection and targeted intervention. Beyond model behaviour, NeuroCogMap improves prediction of human cortical responses during naturalistic language comprehension, with the strongest correspondence in higher-order association cortex. At the cognitive level, its internal signatures expose latent strategies that guide refinements of classical models of human decision-making. Together, these findings establish NeuroCogMap as a system-level framework for mapping functional organization in artificial systems and for relating this organization to human cortical function and cognitive behaviour.
title: NeuroCogMap Reveals Cognitive Organization of Large Language Models
title_translated: NeuroCogMap揭示大型语言模型的认知组织结构
summary_translated: 理解复杂认知功能在人工系统中的组织方式，是解释大型语言模型（LLMs）并与生物认知建立联系的核心。尽管LLMs表现出广泛的类认知行为，但其内部表征是否形成可复现的功能系统以解释行为、失败以及与人类认知的关联仍不明确。本文提出NeuroCogMap，一种受认知神经科学启发的框架，将LLMs的内部特征组织为功能区域，并将其与可解释的功能、认知能力及认知层级相连接。这些区域形成了稳定且语义连贯的组织结构，在不同模型间部分保持一致，并与模型输出功能相关。在此结构中，LLM的主要失败模式，包括幻觉、偏见、拒绝失败和阿谀奉承，对应于表征和行为控制系统的特定破坏，从而为机制引导的检测与针对性干预提供了内部标志。除了模型行为之外，NeuroCogMap还能更准确预测人类在自然语言理解过程中的皮层反应，尤其在高级联合皮层中表现最强。在认知层面，其内部标志揭示了指导经典人类决策模型改进的潜在策略。这些发现共同确立了NeuroCogMap作为映射人工系统功能组织的系统级框架，并将其与人类皮层功能和认知行为相联系。

summary: LLM agents increasingly rely on retrieval buffers to store and reuse past experience, yet the cache management policies governing these buffers remain largely ad-hoc. We formalize this as an online semantic cache replacement problem with switching costs, where items are matched by embedding similarity and hit quality is continuous rather than binary. Through experiments on two datasets from MemoryBench-Full (LoCoMo, DialSim) with 8 replacement policies, we reveal a surprising finding: classic heuristics (LRU, LFU) \emph{consistently underperform} the naive FIFO baseline on semantic workloads, due to the absence of temporal locality and frequency concentration. We propose SOLAR, a learning-augmented framework that derives modification timing from regret accumulation (achieving $\sim$17\% modification rate) and content selection from Bayesian online learning over implicit retrieval feedback. We prove SOLAR achieves a constant competitive ratio $\leq 3$, independent of cache size and horizon (vs.\ $Ω(K)$ for FIFO), and eviction regret $O(\sqrt{KT\log T})$, matching the $Ω(\sqrt{KT})$ lower bound up to logarithmic factors. Experiments demonstrate 5--75\% relative improvement over FIFO at tight cache sizes, with a clearly characterized phase transition at the working set boundary. Synthetic experiments with 5000-item pools further reveal an inverted-U relationship between pool size and retrieval quality, justifying capacity constraints as a retrieval noise phenomenon rather than a storage limitation.
title: When Classic Cache Policies Fail: Learning-Augmented Replacement for Semantic Retrieval Buffers
title_translated: 经典缓存策略失效时：面向语义检索缓冲区的学习增强替换机制
summary_translated: 大型语言模型代理越来越多地依赖检索缓冲区来存储和重用过往经验，但管理这些缓冲区的缓存策略仍 largely 是临时性的。我们将其形式化为一个带有切换成本的在线语义缓存替换问题，其中项目通过嵌入相似性匹配，命中质量为连续值而非二元值。通过对MemoryBench-Full中的两个数据集（LoCoMo, DialSim）进行实验，采用8种替换策略，我们发现一个令人惊讶的结果：经典启发式方法（LRU、LFU）在语义工作负载上始终低于朴素FIFO基线，原因在于缺乏时间局部性和频率集中性。我们提出SOLAR，一种学习增强框架，通过累积遗憾确定修改时机（实现约17%的修改率），并通过贝叶斯在线学习从隐式检索反馈中选择内容。我们证明SOLAR实现了独立于缓存大小和时间范围的常数竞争比≤3（而FIFO为Ω(K)），且淘汰遗憾为O(√KT log T)，在对数因子内达到Ω(√KT)的下界。实验表明，在紧致缓存大小下，SOLAR相比FIFO有5%-75%的相对提升，并在工作集边界处表现出清晰的相变特征。在包含5000个项目的合成实验中进一步揭示了池大小与检索质量之间呈倒U型关系，支持容量限制是检索噪声现象而非存储限制的解释。

summary: Composed Image Retrieval (CIR) retrieves a target image from a reference image and a textual modification. While supervised CIR relies on costly triplets, Zero-Shot CIR (ZS-CIR) alleviates this reliance through proxy tasks trained on image-text pairs. However, existing proxy tasks primarily enhance visual and textual representations to accommodate a predefined composition mechanism such as pseudo-word injection into a frozen text encoder or linear feature arithmetic. As a result, the composition function itself remains unlearned, limiting the model's ability to express diverse and fine-grained semantic modifications. To address this, we propose FoCo, which models composition as two coordinated stages: focusing on modification-relevant visual content, and then completing the target semantics. We realize these through two proxy tasks: text-anchored visual aggregation to selectively gather visual content guided by localized textual semantics, and context-conditioned semantic completion to transform these aggregated visuals with the remaining scene context into a coherent composed representation. The tasks are trained jointly with a cross-instance contrastive objective, encouraging semantic diversity and discouraging shortcut composition strategies. Extensive experiments on four ZS-CIR benchmarks show FoCo's state-of-the-art performance and improved generalization.
title: Learning to Compose: Revisiting Proxy Task Design for Zero-Shot Composed Image Retrieval
title_translated: 学会组合：重新审视零样本组合图像检索中的代理任务设计
summary_translated: 组合图像检索（CIR）从参考图像和文本修改中检索目标图像。虽然监督式CIR依赖于代价高昂的三元组，但零样本CIR（ZS-CIR）通过在图像-文本对上训练的代理任务减轻了这一依赖。然而，现有代理任务主要增强视觉和文本表征，以适应预定义的组合机制，如向冻结文本编码器注入伪词或线性特征算术。因此，组合函数本身未被学习，限制了模型表达多样化和精细语义修改的能力。为解决此问题，我们提出FoCo，将组合建模为两个协调阶段：聚焦于与修改相关的视觉内容，然后完成目标语义。我们通过两个代理任务实现：以文本为锚点的视觉聚合，以局部文本语义引导选择性地聚集视觉内容；以及上下文条件化的语义补全，将聚合后的视觉信息结合剩余场景上下文转化为连贯的组合表示。这两个任务通过跨实例对比目标联合训练，鼓励语义多样性并抑制捷径组合策略。在四个ZS-CIR基准上的大量实验表明，FoCo取得了最先进的性能并提升了泛化能力。

summary: A growing body of literature suggests that training data membership inference problems are fundamentally hard tasks in modern language modeling settings. We argue that output watermarking techniques are the right gadget to make training membership tests for generative models more tractable, based on prior results showing that language models exhibit residual watermark "radioactivity" under partially watermarked training datasets. We pit a watermark-based dataset inference approach head-to-head against traditional loss-based membership inference methods and show that watermarking can achieve comparable membership detection performance when subset exposure is high enough, under an alternate set of assumptions.
title: Watermarking for Proprietary Dataset Protection
comment: 8 pages and 6 figures in the main body; presented at the ICML 2026 Workshop on Trustworthy AI for Good
title_translated: 用于专有数据集保护的水印技术
summary_translated: 越来越多的研究表明，在现代语言建模环境中，训练数据成员身份推断问题本质上是困难的任务。我们认为，输出水印技术是使生成模型的训练成员身份测试更具可处理性的正确工具，基于先前研究显示语言模型在部分水印训练数据下仍表现出残留的水印“放射性”。我们将基于水印的数据集推断方法与传统的基于损失的成员身份推断方法进行直接对比，发现在子集暴露足够高的情况下，水印技术可以达到相当的成员身份检测性能，前提是在一组不同的假设下。

summary: We present a real-time musical interface that converts natural-language scene descriptions into evolving procedural soundscapes. A performer types a prompt such as "warm jazz cafe at midnight" and steers it through direct parameter adjustments - stepping brightness down, switching a rhythm style - each producing a predictable, audible shift without re-prompting. Where GPU-bound text-to-audio systems synthesize monolithic waveforms, our instrument generates human-readable configurations over a categorical schema, enabling fine-grained performer control; most valid combinations are designed to sound musically coherent. Three interchangeable backends - embedding retrieval for sub-second CPU-only use, hosted LLMs via API, and a fine-tuned 270M local model - all emit the same schema. A live generator architecture continuously emits audio while resolving new instructions in the background, crossfading seamlessly when ready; even when an LLM takes 5-12 seconds to respond, the audience hears uninterrupted sound - reframing text-to-music as an ongoing performable stream rather than a one-shot generation. We evaluate text-audio semantic alignment using LAION-CLAP on held-out prompts as a technical proxy, finding that retrieval-based configuration outperforms random valid configurations on this metric, while noting that LAION-CLAP also informed retrieval-map construction. We report performance observations, informal listener feedback, and release materials for the SDK, dataset artifacts, model, and audiovisual performance interface.
title: A Text-Steerable Instrument for Sketching Procedural Soundscapes via Language Models
comment: 10 pages, 7 figures, 2 tables. Accepted to the International Conference on New Interfaces for Musical Expression (NIME 2026), London, UK. Supplementary material included as an appendix. Code and demo: this https URL
title_translated: 一种可由文本操控的乐器：通过语言模型绘制程序化音景
summary_translated: 我们展示了一种实时音乐界面，可将自然语言场景描述转换为不断演化的程序化音景。表演者输入如“午夜温暖的爵士咖啡馆”之类的提示，并通过直接参数调整进行引导——例如降低亮度、切换节奏风格——每次操作都会产生可预测且可听的变化，无需重新提示。与依赖GPU生成单一波形的文本到音频系统不同，我们的乐器生成人类可读的配置，基于分类架构，实现精细的表演者控制；大多数有效组合均设计为具有音乐上的连贯性。三种可互换后端——基于嵌入检索实现亚秒级CPU仅用、通过API调用托管LLM、以及微调的270M本地模型——均输出相同架构。一种实时生成架构持续输出音频，同时在后台解析新指令，准备就绪时无缝交叉淡入；即使LLM响应需5-12秒，观众听到的也是不间断的声音——将文本到音乐重构为持续可表演的流式过程，而非一次性生成。我们使用LAION-CLAP在保留提示上评估文本-音频语义对齐作为技术代理，发现基于检索的配置优于随机有效配置，同时注意到LAION-CLAP也用于指导检索映射构建。我们报告了性能观察、非正式听众反馈，并发布SDK、数据集、模型及音视频表演界面材料。

summary: The bias-reliability tradeoff conjectures that LLM evaluation systems are constrained in (gamma, H, CV) space, where evaluator coupling (gamma), strategy diversity (H), and small-sample measurement reliability (CV(N)) cannot be simultaneously optimized at fixed sample size N. Prior evidence rests on n=5 conditions with complete metrics from a single study. We expand the empirical base to 11 conditions, measuring gamma and H for all 11 (nine with valid weight vectors) and CV(N=5) for seven with sufficient seeds (N >= 5). Five conditions provide the complete (gamma, H, CV) triple. The data confirm the trade-off: conditions with low evaluator coupling (gamma < 0.2) exhibit high measurement noise (CV(N=5) > 1.0), while conditions with strong coupling (gamma > 0.9) achieve low noise (CV(N=5) < 0.16). The correlation r(H, gamma) = -0.989 (n=5, excluding GPT-4o conditions) confirms that evaluator coupling suppresses strategy diversity. Four GPT-4o conditions show gamma=0.000 and H=1.000 across all seeds -- a pattern we attribute to version drift in the June 2026 GPT-4o API. No condition occupies the region {gamma < 0.2, CV(N=5) < 0.3}. We release all per-condition metrics as a standardized benchmark dataset for evaluator comparison.
title: Mapping the Evaluation Frontier: An Empirical Survey of the Bias-Reliability Tradeoff Across Eleven Evaluator-Agent Conditions
title_translated: 评估前沿的测绘：对十一种评估器-智能体条件中偏差-可靠性权衡的实证调查
summary_translated: 该研究探讨了大语言模型评估系统在（gamma, H, CV）空间中的偏差-可靠性权衡假设，即在固定样本量N下，评估器耦合度（gamma）、策略多样性（H）和小样本测量可靠性（CV(N)）无法同时优化。先前证据基于单一研究中n=5种条件的完整指标，本文将实证基础扩展至11种条件，测量了全部11种条件的gamma和H（其中9种具有有效权重向量），以及7种具备足够种子数（N≥5）的条件的CV(N=5)。五种条件提供了完整的（gamma, H, CV）三元组。数据证实了该权衡关系：低评估器耦合度（gamma < 0.2）的条件表现出高测量噪声（CV(N=5) > 1.0），而强耦合度（gamma > 0.9）的条件则实现低噪声（CV(N=5) < 0.16）。相关系数r(H, gamma) = -0.989（n=5，排除GPT-4o条件）表明评估器耦合会抑制策略多样性。四个GPT-4o条件在所有种子中均显示gamma=0.000且H=1.000，我们将其归因于2026年6月GPT-4o API版本漂移。没有条件占据区域{gamma < 0.2, CV(N=5) < 0.3}。研究将所有条件下的指标作为标准化基准数据集发布，用于评估器比较。

summary: When LLM agents use evaluator feedback to adapt their behavior in closed loops, evaluator biases propagate through the agent's strategy distribution -- a phenomenon known as evaluator preference coupling. Prior work has documented coupling across multiple evaluator families and model versions, but the field lacks a standardized protocol that enables third-party researchers to (i) reproduce coupling measurements, (ii) compare results across evaluators and time points, and (iii) detect measurement decay as proprietary evaluators silently update. This paper provides the protocol. We specify EPC (Evaluator Preference Coupling) -- a detailed, RFC-style protocol specification for the four-phase isolation paradigm, covering executor and evaluator configuration, strategy and task design, the TTRL update rule, metric computation (gamma, JSD, ECE, Brier), and output schema. We accompany the protocol with a versioned Reference Snapshot v1.0: coupling measurements for eight evaluator conditions (N=122 unique experimental repetitions across GPT-4o, Qwen, DeepSeek, and others) derived from five independent studies, annotated with evaluator version identifiers, API endpoints, and measurement dates. The snapshot is explicitly time-bound: all values are conditional on specific model versions and are expected to decay as proprietary evaluators update. We define a versioning convention (vX.Y-Z, encoding protocol version, snapshot version, and evaluator generation) and provide a usage guide covering adoption, interpretation, and known pitfalls. The protocol, reference snapshot, and implementation code are released as open infrastructure.
title: EPC: A Standardized Protocol for Measuring Evaluator Preference Dynamics in LLM Agent Systems
title_translated: EPC：LLM智能体系统中评估偏好动态测量的标准协议
summary_translated: 当LLM智能体在闭环中利用评估器反馈调整行为时，评估器偏见会通过智能体策略分布传播——这一现象称为评估偏好耦合。已有研究记录了多种评估器家族和模型版本间的耦合现象，但领域内缺乏标准化协议，以支持第三方研究者（i）复现耦合测量，（ii）跨评估器和时间点比较结果，（iii）检测专有评估器静默更新导致的测量衰减。本文提出EPC（评估偏好耦合）协议：一种详细的RFC风格协议规范，涵盖四阶段隔离范式，包括执行器与评估器配置、策略与任务设计、TTRL更新规则、指标计算（gamma, JSD, ECE, Brier）及输出格式。同时提供版本化参考快照v1.0：来自五个独立研究的八种评估器条件（覆盖GPT-4o、Qwen、DeepSeek等，共122次唯一实验重复）的耦合测量数据，附带评估器版本标识、API端点和测量日期。快照明确限定时间范围：所有数值依赖特定模型版本，预期随专有评估器更新而衰减。定义版本命名约定（vX.Y-Z，编码协议版本、快照版本和评估器生成），并提供使用指南，涵盖采用、解读与已知陷阱。协议、参考快照及实现代码作为开放基础设施发布。

summary: Achieving true artificial general intelligence requires foundation models capable of integrating new modalities without forgetting prior knowledge. However, accommodating continuous generative objectives alongside discrete understanding tasks causes severe gradient conflicts. Existing architectures, including standard Mixture-of-Experts (MoE), are highly susceptible to representation overwriting. Even structurally partitioned paradigms like Mixture-of-Transformers (MoT) remain vulnerable to catastrophic forgetting, severely impeding multimodal scalability. In this work, we introduce Rosetta, a composable native multimodal pretraining framework designed for seamless and non-destructive modality expansion. Rosetta adopts a modular paradigm where core foundational knowledge is preserved within global shared experts, while modality-specific capabilities are distributed across plug-and-play experts. To guarantee non-destructive composition, we propose Momentum-Anchored Orthogonal Projection (MAOP). MAOP leverages the optimizer's momentum state as an implicit semantic anchor, selectively neutralizing conflicting gradient components from new modalities while preserving synergistic updates. Extensive evaluations demonstrate that, while standard MoE and MoT architectures suffer catastrophic forgetting of previously acquired knowledge, Rosetta robustly preserves established language and visual understanding. Furthermore, it delivers superior image generation and unlocks cross-modal synergy, paving the way for truly composable and unified multimodal foundation models. To facilitate further multimodal research, we release our code and checkpoints to the community. Project page at https://rosetta-lmm.github.io/.
title: Rosetta: Composable Native Multimodal Pretraining
title_translated: 罗塞塔：可组合的原生多模态预训练
summary_translated: 实现真正的通用人工智能需要能够无缝整合新模态而不遗忘已有知识的基础模型。然而，在连续生成目标与离散理解任务并存的情况下，现有架构易产生严重的梯度冲突。标准混合专家（MoE）架构极易发生表示覆盖，即使结构上分区的混合变压器（MoT）也仍易受灾难性遗忘影响，严重制约多模态可扩展性。本文提出Rosetta，一种专为无缝、非破坏性模态扩展设计的可组合原生多模态预训练框架。Rosetta采用模块化范式：核心基础知识保留在全局共享专家中，而模态特定能力则分布在即插即用的专家中。为确保非破坏性组合，提出动量锚定正交投影（MAOP）：利用优化器动量状态作为隐式语义锚点，选择性中和新模态带来的冲突梯度分量，同时保留协同更新。大量评估表明，标准MoE与MoT架构在获取新知识时严重遗忘原有语言与视觉理解能力，而Rosetta则稳健保持既有的语言与视觉理解，并实现更优的图像生成效果，激发跨模态协同效应，为真正可组合、统一的多模态基础模型铺平道路。为促进后续多模态研究，项目代码与检查点已向社区开源。项目页面：https://rosetta-lmm.github.io/。

summary: Designing deployable and resilient network topologies from natural language requirements remains a challenging problem in network automation. This work investigates the ability of Large Language Models (LLMs) to generate structurally valid and constraint-compliant network topologies through a constraint-driven pipeline combining hierarchical modeling and systematic validation. The framework is evaluated via a multimodel comparison of proprietary and open-weight LLMs across four realistic network scenarios released as a public dataset. We assess structural correctness using node and edge F1-scores against reference topologies, and evaluate resilience through server and content connectivity metrics. In addition, we analyze common failure modes, including interface mismatches and directional inconsistencies in generated topologies. Overall, this work provides a systematic benchmark for understanding how LLMs handle structural and resilience constraints in topology synthesis, and supports informed model selection for AI-driven network design.
title: An LLM-Based Framework for Intent-Driven Network Topology Design
title_translated: 基于LLM的意图驱动网络拓扑设计框架
summary_translated: 从自然语言需求中设计可部署且具备韧性的网络拓扑仍是网络自动化中的挑战性问题。本研究通过结合层次建模与系统验证的约束驱动流程，探究大语言模型（LLMs）生成结构有效且符合约束的网络拓扑的能力。该框架通过一个公开数据集中的四种真实网络场景，对专有与开源权重LLM进行多模型对比评估。通过与参考拓扑对比节点与边的F1分数评估结构正确性，并通过服务器与内容连通性指标评估韧性。此外，分析常见失败模式，如接口不匹配和生成拓扑中的方向不一致。总体而言，本工作为理解LLM在拓扑合成中处理结构与韧性约束提供了系统性基准，并支持AI驱动网络设计中的模型选型决策。

summary: Current large-language-model (LLM) physics benchmarks are usually scored by answer accuracy, which cannot distinguish genuine reasoning from recall of familiar problem patterns and reveals little about where a model's reasoning breaks down. We introduce an auditable four-stage diagnostic that evaluates whether an LLM can reason inside an unfamiliar physics framework through induction, formulation, prediction, and review. The diagnostic combines locked pre-registrations, fresh sessions between stages, dual-LLM judging, and a human-audit pathway, and we apply it to three parallel physics worlds: a single-equation counterfactual world ($F=mv$), a historical framework (Aristotelian mechanics), and a four-domain counterfactual world (Decay World). Across Claude Opus 4.7, GPT-5.5, and Gemini 3.1 Pro, the three worlds yield composite PASS rates are 6/15, 6/15, and 0/15 respectively (content $\land$ structural for $F=mv$ and Aristotelian, content axis only for Decay World where the structural axis is out of scope). The most pointed empirical pattern is a qualitative-versus-quantitative asymmetry: in Decay World, models almost never predict the wrong direction of change, but frequently compute the wrong ratio by slipping back to standard-physics relations. The protocol also surfaces two methodology findings: LLM-judge reliability does not transfer across frameworks, and Stage 4 self-review is weak in every framework, with the model's own review wrongly reporting no earlier error in at least two-thirds of the trials that actually contained one. We release the full prompts, responses, verdicts, and audit records.
title: Testing Frontier Large Language Models' Physics Literacy in Parallel Physical Worlds
title_translated: 在平行物理世界中测试前沿大语言模型的物理素养
summary_translated: 当前的大语言模型（LLM）物理测评通常仅以答案准确率评分，无法区分真实的推理与对熟悉问题模式的回忆，也无法揭示模型推理在何处失效。为此，本文引入一种可审计的四阶段诊断方法，评估LLM是否能在陌生物理框架中通过归纳、建模、预测与复审完成推理。该诊断结合锁定预注册、各阶段间的新会话、双LLM评判与人工审计路径，应用于三个平行物理世界：单方程反事实世界（F=mv）、历史框架（亚里士多德力学）以及四域反事实世界（衰变世界）。在Claude Opus 4.7、GPT-5.5和Gemini 3.1 Pro中，三个世界的综合通过率分别为6/15、6/15和0/15（F=mv与亚里士多德框架要求内容与结构均通过，衰变世界仅内容轴有效，结构轴超出范围）。最显著的经验模式是定性与定量之间的不对称性：在衰变世界中，模型几乎不会预测错误的变化方向，但频繁因回退到标准物理关系而计算错误的比例。该协议还揭示两个方法论发现：LLM评判可靠性无法跨框架迁移，且在所有框架中第四阶段自审查能力薄弱，至少三分之二的试验中，模型自身审查错误地报告无早期错误，尽管实际存在。全文提示、响应、裁决与审计记录均已发布。

summary: How do two agents invent a shared language from scratch? In a Lewis signaling game, a sender and receiver must coordinate on a code using only their interaction history. We study five memory architectures across varying channel configurations with LLM agents and find that memory architecture matters more than channel capacity. Agents with a persistent private notebook benefit from surplus channel capacity and avoid the high-capacity collapse seen in stateless agents, achieving the most reliable coordination ($0.867 \pm 0.023$ at capacity = 25). Stateless agents peak at moderate capacity and then degrade as the vocabulary grows beyond what a rolling context window can track The notebook externalizes learned conventions, freeing agents from having to re-derive codes each round. An information bottleneck-inspired argument predicts an optimal capacity equal to the number of objects. Instead, the bottleneck (capacity = 8) proves to be a fragility point, and surplus capacity is generally better. We show that channel capacity alone cannot predict coordination; memory architecture determines whether agents turn interaction history into stable conventions, and both dimensions are needed to understand how signals become language.
title: From Signals to Structure: How Memory Architecture Drives Language Emergence in LLM Agents
title_translated: 从信号到结构：记忆架构如何驱动大语言模型智能体中的语言涌现
summary_translated: 两个智能体如何从零开始创造一种共享语言？在Lewis信号博弈中，发送方和接收方必须仅通过交互历史来协调一个编码。我们研究了在不同信道配置下五种记忆架构对大语言模型智能体的影响，发现记忆架构的重要性超过信道容量。具有持久私有笔记的智能体能利用多余的信道容量，避免无状态智能体出现的高容量崩溃问题，在信道容量为25时实现了最可靠的协调（0.867 ± 0.023）。无状态智能体在中等容量时达到峰值，随后随着词汇量超过滚动上下文窗口可追踪范围而性能下降。该笔记将已学习的惯例外部化，使智能体无需每轮重新推导编码。受信息瓶颈启发的论证预测最优容量应等于对象数量，但实际中瓶颈（容量=8）反而成为脆弱点，而冗余容量通常更优。我们证明，仅靠信道容量无法预测协调效果；记忆架构决定了智能体能否将交互历史转化为稳定惯例，理解信号如何演变为语言需要同时考虑这两个维度。

summary: Three of the most popular methods for training language models to reason look like three different tricks. They are not. All three adjust a single number: standard deviation, reflecting how much a prompt's sampled answers disagree. When such a model is trained, it answers each problem many times, and an automatic checker marks every answer right or wrong. The standard deviation of those marks measures the disagreement: largest when the answers split evenly between right and wrong, and zero when they all agree. Group Relative Policy Optimization (GRPO) divides by this number, GRPO Done Right (Dr. GRPO) drops the division, and Decoupled Clip and Dynamic Sampling Policy Optimization (DAPO) discards the groups where it is zero. Each is presented as its own fix, yet this paper proves they are three settings of one dial. That dial is not cosmetic: for right-or-wrong rewards, the disagreement is exactly the size of the training update, the group-standard-deviation identity. A split group teaches the most, while a unanimous group teaches nothing and falls silent. The same result says which problems deserve the most weight and how many tries each one needs. This paper confirms the intuition on a large real difficulty dataset (Big-Math) and in a controlled training run. What looks like a harmless normalization step is the dial that decides where learning happens and how strongly.
title: GRPO, Dr. GRPO, and DAPO Are Three Operations on One Number: The Group-Standard-Deviation Identity
comment: 18 pages, 10 figures, 4 tables. Code and data: this https URL
title_translated: GRPO、Dr. GRPO 和 DAPO 是对同一数值的三种操作：群组标准差恒等式
summary_translated: 三种训练语言模型进行推理的流行方法看似是三种不同的技巧，实则不然。它们都调整同一个数值：标准差，反映提示词采样答案之间的分歧程度。当此类模型训练时，会多次回答每个问题，自动检查器标记每个答案的正确或错误。这些标记的标准差衡量分歧程度：当答案在正确与错误间平均分配时最大，全部一致时为零。群组相对策略优化（GRPO）对此值进行除法，正确的GRPO（Dr. GRPO）省略除法，而解耦剪裁与动态采样策略优化（DAPO）则丢弃标准差为零的组。每种方法都被视为独立修复方案，但本文证明它们实际上是同一旋钮的三种设置。该旋钮并非装饰性：对于对错奖励，分歧程度恰好等于训练更新的大小，即群组标准差恒等式。分裂的组最具教学价值，而一致的组则不提供任何学习信号并趋于沉默。相同结果还指明哪些问题应获得更高权重以及每个问题需要多少次尝试。本文在大型真实难度数据集（Big-Math）和受控训练实验中验证了这一直觉。看似无害的归一化步骤，实则是决定学习发生位置与强度的关键旋钮。

summary: As computing education expands beyond traditional programming into operational domains such as systems administration and command-line environments, existing pedagogical frameworks struggle to capture a dimension that is critical in these contexts: the real-world consequences of learner actions. Existing cognitive taxonomies classify learning objectives by mental operations but do not account for system impact, leaving a critical gap in command-line education where conceptually simple commands can have severe consequences. This work presents CogTax, a four-level cognitive taxonomy that integrates two dimensions: cognitive complexity, derived from Bloom's Revised Taxonomy, and operational impact, which distinguishes observational, reversible, structural, and administrative operations. The four progressive levels range from safe read-only inspection to advanced system management requiring integration of multiple abstract models. Then, the taxonomy level is defined as the maximum of these dimensions, ensuring that both conceptual understanding and operational awareness are addressed. CogTax gives instructors a principled framework for sequencing course material and calibrating assessment difficulty, and gives students an explicit reference for self-assessment and gap identification. To demonstrate that taxonomy levels are automatically assignable, making the framework scalable without manual expert annotation, a classifier that combines syntactic representations derived from abstract syntax trees with semantic embeddings is trained. Evaluated on 585 expert-annotated Linux/bash commands, this combined approach achieves 89% accuracy, outperforming either representation alone, and demonstrates cross-language extensibility through structural equivalences across command languages.
title: CogTax: A Four-Level Cognitive Taxonomy for Command-Line Computing Education
title_translated: CogTax：命令行计算教育的四层认知分类体系
summary_translated: 随着计算教育从传统编程扩展到系统管理与命令行环境等操作领域，现有教学框架难以捕捉这些情境中至关重要的维度：学习者行为的真实世界后果。现有的认知分类体系按心理操作对学习目标进行分类，但未考虑系统影响，导致命令行教育中存在关键空白——概念上简单的命令可能带来严重后果。本文提出CogTax，一种融合两个维度的四层认知分类体系：认知复杂性（源自布卢姆修订版分类）和操作影响，区分观察性、可逆性、结构性和管理性操作。四个递进层级从安全只读检查延伸至需整合多个抽象模型的高级系统管理。分类层级定义为这两个维度的最大值，确保概念理解与操作意识均被覆盖。CogTax为教师提供了课程内容编排与评估难度校准的理论框架，也为学生提供了自我评估与差距识别的明确参考。为证明分类层级可自动分配，从而实现无需人工专家标注的可扩展性，我们训练了一个结合抽象语法树派生的句法表示与语义嵌入的分类器。在585个专家标注的Linux/bash命令上评估，该组合方法达到89%准确率，优于单一表示，并通过跨命令语言的结构等价性展示了可扩展性。

summary: We study a finite-state symbolic controller for systems in which the admissible visible transitions are fixed in advance and each visible state carries a minimum dwell requirement. The resulting model, which we call a destination-labeled self-looping system with dwell (DLSL system), records the visible graph together with local decision maps; dwell memory appears only after phase expansion.   The main structural issue is that, once dwell is imposed, the current visible state no longer determines whether a departure is allowed. This leads to the converse problem: which deterministic transducers arise as phase-expanded realizations of DLSL systems over a fixed visible graph? We show that the answer is exactly the class of fiber-linear graph-respecting transducers. Under natural reachability and realizable-departure assumptions, equivalent accessible realizations over the same visible graph are isomorphic; in particular, the visible transduction determines the dwell vector and the local decision maps. We also prove that any graph-preserving deterministic realization enforcing dwell values $(d_i)$ requires exactly $\sum_i d_i$ control states. Finally, we give an $O(|Q||Ω|)$ recognition and reconstruction procedure, and extend the analysis to an edge-entry variant in which transitions may enter interior phases of successor fibers.
title: Destination-Labeled Self-Looping Systems with Dwell: Intrinsic Characterization, Realization Cost, and Recognition
title_translated: 带停留时间的目的地标记自环系统：内在表征、实现成本与识别
summary_translated: 我们研究了一类有限状态符号控制器，其允许的可见转移在事先固定，且每个可见状态带有最小停留时间要求。由此产生的模型称为带停留时间的目的地标记自环系统（DLSL系统），记录可见图及局部决策映射；停留记忆仅在相位展开后出现。主要结构问题是：一旦施加停留时间，当前可见状态不再决定是否允许离开。这引出了反问题：在固定可见图上，哪些确定性转换器可作为DLSL系统的相位展开实现？我们证明答案正是纤维线性图保持转换器这一类。在自然可达性和可实现出发假设下，同一可见图上的等价可达实现彼此同构；特别地，可见转换决定了停留向量和局部决策映射。我们还证明，任何保持图结构的确定性实现若强制执行停留值$(d_i)$，则恰好需要$\	extstyle\sum_i d_i$个控制状态。最后，我们给出一个$O(|Q||Ω|)$的识别与重构算法，并将分析扩展至边进入变体，其中转移可进入后继纤维的内部阶段。

summary: Long-term conversational agents are expected to remember past interactions, but memory is useful only when the right evidence is recalled for the right user. Existing memory-augmented LLM agents have made progress in building compact memory banks, yet retrieval is still often driven by query-centered similarity or fixed ranking rules, leaving user-conditioned relevance underexplored.To address this gap, we propose Profile-guided Personalized Retrieval Optimization (PPRO), a retrieval-centric framework that makes memory retrieval both user-aware and optimizable.PPRO builds episodic and semantic memory banks from dialogue histories and derives a user profile from accumulated memories.The profile serves as an explicit personalized prior in memory ranking, allowing retrieval to account for stable user attributes, preferences, and relationships.PPRO further trains a query rewriter with Group Relative Policy Optimization, using both evidence retrieval quality and downstream answer quality as feedback while keeping the memory banks and answer model fixed.Experiments on LoCoMo and LongMemEval-S show consistent gains over training-free memory systems and training-based baselines.Ablation studies further show that both profile-guided ranking and retrieval-oriented rewriting contribute substantially to performance, highlighting retrieval optimization as a key factor in personalized long-term memory use.
title: Learning User-Aware Recall: Personalized Retrieval in Long-Term Conversational Memory
title_translated: 学习用户感知的召回：长期对话记忆中的个性化检索
summary_translated: 长期对话智能体应能记住过往互动，但记忆只有在为特定用户召回恰当证据时才有效。现有增强记忆的大语言模型智能体在构建紧凑记忆库方面取得进展，但检索仍常由查询中心相似性或固定排序规则驱动，用户条件相关性尚未充分探索。为填补这一空白，我们提出基于用户画像的个性化检索优化（PPRO），一种以检索为核心的框架，使记忆检索兼具用户感知性与可优化性。PPRO从对话历史构建情景记忆与语义记忆库，并从累积记忆中推导用户画像。该画像作为记忆排序中的显式个性化先验，使检索能够考虑用户的稳定属性、偏好与关系。PPRO进一步使用群组相对策略优化训练查询重写器，同时以证据检索质量与下游回答质量为反馈，保持记忆库与答案模型不变。在LoCoMo和LongMemEval-S上的实验显示，PPRO持续优于无训练记忆系统与基于训练的基线。消融研究进一步表明，基于画像的排序与面向检索的重写均显著提升性能，凸显检索优化在个性化长期记忆使用中的核心作用。

summary: Conversational recommender systems (CRSs) are a core component of next-generation intelligent recommender systems because they enable users to actively elicit preferences, clarify intentions, and adapt recommendations in real time. However, there are two key obstacles in the CRS domain: evaluation and access to training data. Evaluating CRSs through real human studies is more critical than for traditional recommender systems, yet such studies are both costly and time-consuming. Moreover, CRS interaction data are often difficult to obtain for model training due to privacy concerns. Large language model (LLM)-based user simulators have shown promise in addressing both challenges by generating synthetic user interactions for evaluation and training. However, existing approaches suffer from systematic positive bias, data leakage, and limited behavioral diversity, and they rely on brittle manual prompt engineering that requires extensive domain expertise. In this paper, we propose a framework to automatically optimize prompts for LLM-based user simulators in CRSs, simultaneously mitigating these issues. Experimental results demonstrate that the proposed framework achieves improved behavioral alignment with human interaction patterns compared to baseline methods across diverse prompt settings.
title: Prompt Optimization for User Simulation in Conversational Recommender Systems: A Multi-Objective Framework
comment: to be published in 2026 IEEE 42nd International Conference on Data Engineering Workshops (ICDEW)
title_translated: 对话推荐系统中用户模拟的提示优化：一种多目标框架
summary_translated: 对话推荐系统（CRS）是下一代智能推荐系统的核心组成部分，因为它使用户能够主动表达偏好、澄清意图，并实时调整推荐内容。然而，CRS领域存在两个关键障碍：评估困难和训练数据获取难。通过真实人类实验评估CRS比传统推荐系统更为重要，但此类研究成本高且耗时长。此外，由于隐私问题，CRS交互数据往往难以用于模型训练。基于大语言模型（LLM）的用户模拟器在解决这两个挑战方面展现出潜力，可通过生成合成用户交互数据用于评估和训练。然而，现有方法存在系统性正向偏差、数据泄露以及行为多样性有限的问题，且依赖于脆弱的手动提示工程，需要大量领域专业知识。本文提出一种自动优化LLM-based用户模拟器提示的框架，同时缓解上述问题。实验结果表明，所提出的框架在多种提示设置下相较于基线方法，实现了与人类交互模式更优的行为一致性。

summary: Multimodal large language models (MLLMs) provide new opportunities for blind and low vision (BLV) people to access visual information in their daily lives. However, these models often produce errors that are difficult to detect without sight, posing safety and social risks in scenarios from medication identification to outfit selection. While BLV MLLM users use creative workarounds such as cross-checking between tools and consulting sighted individuals, these approaches are often time-consuming and impractical. We explore how systematically surfacing variations across multiple MLLM responses can support BLV users to detect unreliable information without visually inspecting the image. We contribute a design space for eliciting and presenting variations in MLLM descriptions, a prototype system implementing three variation presentation styles, and findings from a user study with 15 BLV participants. Our results demonstrate that presenting variations significantly increases users' ability to identify unreliable claims (by 4.9x using our approach compared to single descriptions) and significantly decreases perceived reliability of MLLM responses. 14 of 15 participants preferred seeing variations of MLLM responses over a single description, and all expressed interest in using our system for tasks from understanding a tornado's path to posting an image on social media.
title: Surfacing Variations to Calibrate Perceived Reliability of MLLM-generated Image Descriptions
title_translated: 揭示多模态大模型生成图像描述中的差异以校准感知可靠性
summary_translated: 多模态大语言模型（MLLM）为盲人和低视力（BLV）人群在日常生活中获取视觉信息提供了新机遇。然而，这些模型常产生难以察觉的错误，尤其在药物识别到穿搭选择等场景中，可能带来安全与社会风险。尽管BLV用户会采用诸如跨工具交叉验证或咨询 sighted 人士等创造性应对策略，但这些方法往往耗时且不切实际。我们探索了如何系统性地呈现多个MLLM响应之间的差异，以帮助BLV用户在无法视觉检查图像的情况下检测不可靠信息。我们提出了一个用于激发和展示MLLM描述差异的设计空间，实现了一个包含三种差异呈现风格的原型系统，并通过15名BLV用户的用户研究获得了相关发现。结果表明，呈现差异显著提升了用户识别不可靠陈述的能力（使用本方法比单一描述提升4.9倍），并显著降低了对MLLM响应的感知可靠性。14位参与者更倾向于查看MLLM响应的差异而非单一描述，且所有参与者均表示愿意将该系统用于从理解龙卷风路径到在社交媒体上发布图片等任务。
